title: Bridge Essay: Time varying dynamics of hallucinations in clinical and non-clinical voice-heare

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on acontinuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

**Time varying dynamics of hallucinations in clinical and non-clinical voice-hearers.**. Neuroimage Clin.. PMID: 36805417. DOI: 10.1016/j.nicl.2023.103351. Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) are frequently associated with psychotic disorders, yet also occur in non-clinical voice-hearers. AVH in this group are similar to those within clinical voice-hearers in terms of several phenomenological aspects, but non-clinical voice-hearers report to have more control over their AVH and attribute less emotional valence to them.
